Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: food dryers [300425828], culinary equipment for preparing and cooking food [300199784], culinary equipment [300199765], equipment by context [300239170], equipment [300122241], Tools and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300022238], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Non-Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: trays [300043071], containers by form [300194507], containers (receptacles) [300197197], Containers (hierarchy name) [300045611], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Trays or gridded racks used for drying fruits or nuts; traditionally made of natural fibers or wooden slats woven with open spaces for air circulation, and may have a handle.

Source.Source_ID: 2000106166/Nomenclature for Museum Cataloging / Nomenclature pour le catalogage translation project (2016-)
2000106168/Parks Canada Descriptive Dictionary of Objects / Dictionnaire descriptif d’objets (c 1996)

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Bandejas usadas para secar frutas o nueces; tradicionalmente hechas de fibras naturales o tablillas de madera entretejidas con espacios abiertos para la circulación del aire, y que pueden tener un asa.

Schalen die worden gebruikt om er fruit of noten op te drogen; traditioneel vervaardigd van natuurlijke vezels of houten latten, waarin openingen zijn verwerkt voor de luchtcirculatie en soms met een handvat.

Trays used for drying fruits or nuts; traditionally made of natural fibers or wooden slats woven with open spaces for air circulation, and may have a handle.Tablett, das benutzt wird um Früchte oder Nüsse zu trocknen; traditionell aus Naturfasern oder geflochtenen Holzleisten mit offenen Flächen für die Luftzirkulation hergestellt, es kann einen Griff besitzen.

Term_Source.Page: Kathy Beebee, Curator, Berkshire Museum, Pittsfield, Ma., 10/95

Source.Source_ID: 2000106166/Nomenclature for Museum Cataloging / Nomenclature pour le catalogage translation project (2016-)
2000027581/Gould, Early American Wooden Ware, rev. ed. (1962)
